summary: Middle East conflict (Iran) is the primary flashpoint: US blockade of Iranian ports threatens Strait of Hormuz and global hydrocarbon supply. IMF projects global growth of only 3.1% and headline inflation of 4.4% in 2026 assuming a short-lived conflict with ~19% energy price spike. US also involved in Venezuela, and strategic competition over Greenland intensifying. Governments increasingly using tariffs, export controls, and industrial subsidies reshaping supply chains.
